title: Twist-Angle Torus Tiles the Brillouin Zone Exactly Once
summary: Summing over discrete crystal momenta and integrating the twist angles over 0 <= alpha_i < 2pi is exactly the same as integrating over the full Brillouin zone.
assumptions: The finite system has periodicities L_1 and L_2 with discrete momenta p_i = (2 pi s_i + alpha_i)/L_i.
The integrand is periodic in the Brillouin zone.
regime: Finite periodic sample used in the many-body to band-theory equivalence argument.
scope: Key counting step in Witten's proof that the twist-angle Chern number equals the band Chern number.

math_expressions.context: Allowed momenta in the finite periodic system with twists.
Twist-angle integration plus discrete summation gives the Brillouin-zone integral.

symbols.meaning: integers labeling the discrete momenta of the finite periodic system

failure_modes: If the finite-size momentum set does not uniformly sample the Brillouin zone, the reorganization identity fails.
retrieval_hints: Use when proving hat k' = k' without skipping the finite-size tiling step.
